title: A foreign neighbor who became a friend
summary: Describe a neighbor from another country who became your friend.
topic: A foreign neighbor who became a friend
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences about a neighbor from another country who became your friend. Describe how you met and what you share. Use present simple and past simple.
hintStarters: A new neighbor moved into the flat next to mine. He is from…
sampleText: A new neighbor moved into the flat next to mine in Edinburgh last winter. His name is Tomek and he is from Poland. He is thirty years old. The first week, he only said hello in the corridor. He was a little shy because his English was new. One evening, he knocked on my door to borrow sugar. I gave him sugar and a cup of tea. We talked for one hour with simple words and a phone translator. Now we are friends. We meet on Sundays. He cooks Polish food, and I cook food from my country. We do not speak the same first language, but we learn a few words from each other every week. He is patient with me when I forget a word. We share photos of our families. I help him with English, and he tells me about life in Poland. A foreign neighbor is not a stranger. He can become a real friend.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: ứng dụng dịch trên điện thoại

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Mixing past and present
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Có thể kể quá khứ (lúc gặp nhau) rồi chuyển sang hiện tại (tình bạn hôm nay): 'He moved into the flat last winter' → 'Now we are friends', 'We meet on Sundays'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: You can tell the past (when you met) and then move to the present (the friendship today): 'He moved into the flat last winter' → 'Now we are friends', 'We meet on Sundays'.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'each other' for two-way actions
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'each other' khi cả hai người làm chung một việc cho nhau: 'we learn a few words from each other every week'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'each other' when two people do the same action for one another: 'we learn a few words from each other every week'.

sampleTranslationVi: Một người hàng xóm mới dọn vào căn hộ kế bên tôi ở Edinburgh vào mùa đông năm ngoái. Anh ấy tên là Tomek và đến từ Ba Lan. Anh ấy ba mươi tuổi. Tuần đầu, anh chỉ chào trong hành lang. Anh hơi nhút nhát vì tiếng Anh còn mới. Một buổi tối, anh gõ cửa nhà tôi để mượn đường. Tôi đưa anh đường và một tách trà. Chúng tôi nói chuyện một giờ bằng những từ đơn giản và một ứng dụng dịch trên điện thoại. Bây giờ chúng tôi là bạn. Chúng tôi gặp nhau vào Chủ Nhật. Anh nấu món Ba Lan, và tôi nấu món của nước tôi. Chúng tôi không nói cùng tiếng mẹ đẻ, nhưng chúng tôi học vài từ của nhau mỗi tuần. Anh kiên nhẫn với tôi khi tôi quên từ. Chúng tôi chia sẻ ảnh gia đình. Tôi giúp anh tiếng Anh, và anh kể tôi nghe về cuộc sống ở Ba Lan. Một người hàng xóm nước ngoài không phải là người lạ. Anh ấy có thể trở thành một người bạn thật sự.

mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'in Edinburgh last winter'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the city in the first sentence.

mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'he is from Poland'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Why did Tomek knock on the door?
options: To ask for help with the heater
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'he knocked on my door to borrow sugar'.

mcQuestions.prompt: What did the writer give him?
options: Sugar and a cup of tea
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'I gave him sugar and a cup of tea'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find what was given to Tomek.

mcQuestions.prompt: How long did they talk that first evening?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'We talked for one hour'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the length of the first chat.

mcQuestions.prompt: How did they understand each other?
options: With simple words and a phone translator
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'with simple words and a phone translator'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the two tools they used.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does Tomek cook on Sundays?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'He cooks Polish food'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the food connected with Poland.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence is correct past simple?
options: He knocked on my door to borrow sugar.
He knock on my door to borrow sugar.
He knocking on my door to borrow sugar.
He knocks on my door to borrow sugar.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for the -ed ending in the past.

options: We learn a few words each other every week.
We learn a few words from each other every week.
We learn a few words from each others every week.
We learn each other a few words every week.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'from each other' is the correct phrase, no -s.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for the right preposition before 'each other'.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does the writer think about a foreign neighbor?
options: A foreign neighbor is always a stranger
A foreign neighbor can become a real friend
A foreign neighbor is hard to talk to
A foreign neighbor never stays long
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'A foreign neighbor is not a stranger. He can become a real friend'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look at the last two sentences.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why is it sometimes hard to make friends with people who do not speak our language well? What helps two people become close even when their words are limited?
